title: Campus Tour
transcript:
A: Welcome to our campus tour! I'm Sarah.
B: Hi Sarah! I'm Tom, excited to see the campus.
A: Let's start with the library—over two million volumes.
B: Impressive! Digital resources too?
A: Absolutely, hundreds of academic databases.
B: Study spaces?
A: Quiet floors, group rooms, and a cafe.
B: Perfect. Next stop?
A: The student center with restaurants, shops, and 300+ organizations.
B: Can't wait to explore!
transcript_cn:
A: 欢迎参加校园参观！我是莎拉。
B: 你好莎拉！我是汤姆，很期待。
A: 从图书馆开始——超过两百万册。
B: 有数字资源吗？
A: 当然，数百个学术数据库。
B: 学习空间？
A: 安静楼层、小组室和咖啡馆。
B: 完美。下一站？
A: 学生中心，有餐厅、商店和300+组织。
B: 迫不及待！

title: Library Book Return
transcript:
A: I'd like to return these books.
B: Student ID please. You have a $5 fine for a book returned 3 days late.
A: Can I pay now?
B: Yes. We also waive fines if you volunteer 2 hours here.
A: Great option! When can I volunteer?
B: This Saturday 9-11 AM.
A: I'll sign up. Thanks!
B: You can also renew online up to twice.
transcript_cn:
A: 我想还书。
B: 请出示学生证。有一本书逾期3天，罚款5美元。
A: 现在能付吗？
B: 可以。做2小时志愿者可减免罚款。
A: 好选择！什么时候？
B: 本周六9-11点。
A: 我报名。谢谢！
B: 也可以网上续借最多两次。

title: Dormitory Move-in
transcript:
A: Moving into Room 302 today.
B: Welcome to Green Hall! Need your housing contract and ID.
A: Here they are. When do I get my key?
B: After paperwork. You'll also get a key card.
A: Dorm rules?
B: Quiet hours 10PM-7AM weekdays. No cooking in rooms, shared kitchens on each floor.
A: Visitors?
B: Until 10PM. Overnight guests registered 24 hours in advance.
A: Mail?
B: Lobby mailbox, number matches your room.

B: 工作日安静时间晚10到早7。房间禁止烹饪，每层有共享厨房。

B: 到晚10点。过夜客人提前24小时登记。
A: 邮件？
B: 大厅邮箱，号码对应房间。

title: Course Registration
transcript:
A: Professor, I can't register for your advanced psychology course.
B: What's wrong, Lisa?
A: System says I need permission, but I took the prerequisite.
B: You took Psych 201, but this needs Psych 202 too.
A: Any way to still take it?
B: Students with strong records can take it concurrently. Your GPA is 3.7.
A: What do I need?
B: Override form with my signature, and attend the first two weeks.
A: I'll get the form today. Thank you!
transcript_cn:
A: 教授，我注册不了高级心理学。
B: 怎么了丽莎？
A: 系统说要许可，但我修了先修课。
B: 你修了心理学201，但这门还需要202。
A: 还有办法吗？
B: 成绩优秀的可以同时修。你GPA 3.7。
A: 我要做什么？
B: 填超员申请表让我签字，并参加前两周。
A: 我今天就去领表。谢谢！

title: Meal Plan
transcript:
A: I'd like a meal plan. What's available?
B: Basic: 10 meals/week. Standard: 14. Premium: unlimited.
A: Prices?
B: Basic $1500/semester, Standard $2000, Premium $2500.
A: Standard works. Can I change later?
B: Within first 2 weeks each semester.
A: Does it cover the cafe?
B: Standard and Premium include dining dollars. Standard has $100/semester.
A: I'll go with Standard.
transcript_cn:
A: 我想办餐饮计划。有什么？
B: 基本10餐/周，标准14，高级无限。
A: 价格？
B: 基本1500/学期，标准2000，高级2500。
A: 标准适合。可以改吗？
B: 每学期前两周内。
A: 含咖啡馆吗？
B: 标准和高级含餐饮额度。标准100/学期。
A: 我选标准。

title: Part-time Job
transcript:
A: Is the research assistant position still available?
B: Yes. Graduate or undergraduate?
A: Junior in biology. Lab experience since sophomore year.
B: Great. It involves data collection for our marine biology project.
A: Hours per week?
B: About 15. Flexible, but Wednesday afternoon lab meetings required.
A: Pay?
B: $14/hour for undergrads, $18 for grads.
A: How to apply?
B: Resume and statement of interest by next Friday.
transcript_cn:
A: 研究助理还有吗？
B: 有。研究生还是本科生？
A: 生物大三。大二起有实验经验。
B: 好。涉及海洋生物学数据收集。
A: 每周几小时？
B: 约15。灵活，但周三下午要开会。
A: 薪资？
B: 本科14美元/时，研究生18。
A: 怎么申请？
B: 下周五前交简历和兴趣说明。

title: Roommate Conflict
transcript:
A: Mike, can we talk? You playing music late bothers me.
B: Sorry! I'll use headphones after 11 from now on.
A: Thanks. Can we agree on a cleaning schedule?
B: Take turns every other week?
A: Works. Also, whoever notices trash is full takes it out.
B: Reasonable. Glad we talked.
A: Communication is key.
B: Absolutely. Let's address things right away.
transcript_cn:

B: 对不起！11点后我戴耳机。

B: 轮流隔周怎么样？
A: 可以。谁看到垃圾满了谁倒。
B: 合理。很高兴我们谈了。
A: 沟通很重要。
B: 绝对，有问题立刻说。

title: Scholarship
transcript:
A: I'd like to apply for the Dean's Scholarship.
B: GPA 3.5+ and leadership in extracurriculars required.
A: My GPA is 3.8, I'm debate club president.
B: Promising. Need transcript, personal statement, and 2 recommendation letters.
A: Deadline?
B: March 15th. Reviews in April, results by May 1st.
A: How much?
B: Full tuition for one year, about $20,000.
A: Incredible! I'll apply.
B: Start your statement early—it carries weight.

B: 3月15日。4月审核，5月1日前公布。
A: 金额？
B: 一学年全额学费，约2万美元。
A: 太棒了！我一定申请。
B: 尽早开始写陈述。

title: Study Abroad
transcript:
A: Tell me about the Japan exchange program.

B: Recommended but not required. Many courses in English.
A: Cost?
B: Pay regular tuition here. Extra for airfare, housing, living—about $5000/semester.
A: Study abroad scholarships?
B: Grants from $1000-$3000 based on need and merit.

B: 付本校学费。额外约5000美元/学期。
A: 有奖学金吗？
B: 根据需求和成绩1000-3000美元。

transcript:

B: 3 stops: student center, engineering, sports complex.
A: Weekend schedule?
B: Every 40 minutes. Last shuttle 8PM weekends, 11PM weekdays.

B: 3站：学生中心、工程楼、体育中心。

title: History of the Internet
transcript: The internet began as a military project in the 1960s when the US Defense Department developed ARPANET. By the 1970s, email was invented. In 1989, Tim Berners-Lee proposed the World Wide Web. By the mid-1990s, browsers brought the internet into homes worldwide. Today, over four billion people use it daily. From social media to online shopping, the internet reshapes our society profoundly.

title: Climate Change
transcript: Rising global temperatures cause glaciers to melt at unprecedented rates, raising sea levels. Extreme weather events—hurricanes, droughts, heat waves—are more frequent. Wildlife habitats are disrupted. The Paris Climate Accord aims to limit warming to well below 2°C above pre-industrial levels. Achieving this requires cooperation from all nations and transition to renewable energy.

title: Benefits of Exercise
transcript: Regular exercise prevents chronic diseases—heart disease, diabetes, certain cancers. It strengthens muscles and bones, improves cardiovascular health, and boosts immunity. Exercise also benefits mental health: reducing depression and anxiety, improving mood and cognition. Studies show 30 minutes of brisk walking 5 times weekly significantly improves health. The key is finding activities you enjoy and making them routine.

title: The Water Cycle
transcript: The water cycle describes water's continuous movement. It begins with evaporation—sun heats water into vapor rising into the atmosphere. Vapor cools and condenses into clouds. When droplets grow large enough, they fall as precipitation—rain, snow, sleet, or hail. Some water flows as surface runoff back to oceans. Some infiltrates soil as groundwater. The cycle distributes fresh water and regulates global temperatures.
transcript_cn: 水循环描述水的持续运动。从蒸发开始——太阳将水加热成蒸气上升。蒸气冷却凝结成云。水滴足够大时以雨雪等形式降落。一些水作为地表径流回到海洋，一些渗入土壤成为地下水。水循环分配淡水并调节全球温度。

title: Cultural Diversity
transcript: Culturally diverse cities tend to be more innovative and economically productive. Different traditions, cuisines, and perspectives benefit everyone. However, language barriers and cultural misunderstandings can create social tensions. Successful multicultural cities invest in language education and intercultural dialogue. The key is mutual respect and willingness to learn from one another.

title: Importance of Sleep
transcript: Adults need 7-9 hours of sleep nightly. Sleep repairs tissues, strengthens immunity, and consolidates memories. Lack of sleep impairs concentration, weakens immunity, and increases risk of obesity and heart disease. Experts recommend consistent schedules, comfortable environments, and avoiding electronic devices before bedtime. Physical activity and limiting caffeine also improve sleep quality.
transcript_cn: 成人每晚需7-9小时睡眠。睡眠修复组织、增强免疫、巩固记忆。睡眠不足损害注意力、削弱免疫、增加肥胖和心脏病风险。专家建议一致的睡眠时间表、舒适环境和睡前避免电子设备。运动和限制咖啡因也有帮助。

title: AI and Jobs
transcript: AI threatens to automate many jobs—manufacturing, transportation, customer service are most vulnerable. Studies estimate up to 30% of current jobs could be automated within two decades. However, AI also creates new job types. Workers need adaptability and lifelong learning. Skills AI can't easily replicate—creativity, emotional intelligence, critical thinking—will be crucial for competitiveness.
transcript_cn: AI威胁使许多工作自动化——制造业、交通和客服最脆弱。研究估计20年内高达30%的现有工作可能被自动化。但AI也创造新工作类型。工人需要适应性和终身学习。AI难以复制的技能——创造力、情商和批判性思维——对竞争力至关重要。

title: Power of Reading
transcript: Reading improves vocabulary, enhances cognition, and increases empathy. Fiction readers score higher on social cognition tests. Just 6 minutes of reading can reduce stress by up to 68%. For children, early reading habits correlate strongly with academic success. Parents can foster this by reading aloud and providing books. In the digital age, reading's benefits remain unchanged.

title: Urbanization
transcript: Over half the world's population lives in cities, expected to reach nearly 70% by 2050. Cities offer economic opportunities, better healthcare, and education. But rapid urbanization creates housing shortages, traffic congestion, and resource pressure. Green buildings, efficient public transport, and smart city technologies are solutions for livable urban environments.

title: Social Media Impact
transcript: Social media connects billions worldwide but has complex impacts. Positives: social connections, rapid emergency information, giving voice to marginalized communities. Negatives: studies link excessive use to anxiety, depression, and loneliness, especially in young people. Misinformation and online harassment are significant concerns. Finding healthy balance is key to digital well-being.

title: Science of Memory
transcript: Memory involves three stages: encoding, storage, and retrieval. Short-term memory holds information for seconds to minutes; long-term memory can last a lifetime. Stress, sleep, nutrition, and age affect memory. Regular exercise, adequate sleep, and mental stimulation help maintain and improve memory function throughout life.

transcript: Explorer Four rover landed on Mars Tuesday morning, sending high-resolution images. Scientists excited about mineral deposits indicating ancient water. Two-year mission to collect soil samples and analyze geology. Program invested over $3 billion in Mars exploration over the past decade. Follow-up mission planned for 2029 to return samples.

transcript: For the first time, renewable energy generated more electricity than fossil fuels in the EU. Wind and solar: 42%. Coal and gas: 39%. Nuclear: 19%. title: Medical Breakthrough
transcript: Stanford researchers announced NeuroClear slows Alzheimer's cognitive decline by 40% in clinical trials with 3,000+ participants. The drug clears amyloid plaques from the brain. Side effects are mild—headaches and nausea. Not a cure but slows progression. Regulatory approval expected within 18 months.
transcript_cn: 斯坦福研究人员宣布NeuroClear在3000+人试验中将阿尔茨海默认知衰退减缓40%。该药清除大脑淀粉样斑块。副作用轻微——头痛和恶心。非治愈药物但减缓进展。预计18个月内获监管批准。

sentences.text: Stanford researchers announced NeuroClear slows Alzheimer's cognitive decline by 40% in clinical trials with 3,000+ participants. The drug clears amyloid plaques from the brain. Side effects are mild—headaches and nausea. Not a cure but slows progression. Regulatory approval expected within 18 months.

title: Maslow's Hierarchy
transcript: Maslow proposed in 1943 that human needs form a pyramid. Base: physiological needs—food, water, shelter. Second: safety. Third: love and belonging. Fourth: esteem. Top: self-actualization. Lower needs must be met before higher ones. Criticism: too rigid, doesn't account for cultural differences.
transcript_cn: 马斯洛1943年提出人类需求呈金字塔。底部：生理需求。第二层：安全。第三层：爱和归属。第四层：尊重。顶部：自我实现。低层需求须先满足。批评：过于僵化，未考虑文化差异。

sentences.text: Maslow proposed in 1943 that human needs form a pyramid. Base: physiological needs—food, water, shelter. Second: safety. Third: love and belonging. Fourth: esteem. Top: self-actualization. Lower needs must be met before higher ones. Criticism: too rigid, doesn't account for cultural differences.

title: Public Speaking
transcript: Five principles: 1) Preparation—research, organize, practice. 2) Know your audience—tailor your message. 3) Manage anxiety—deep breathing, visualization, positive self-talk. 4) Body language—eye contact, natural gestures, purposeful movement. 5) Engage your audience—stories, examples, questions. Speeches that feel like conversations are most effective.
transcript_cn: 五个原则：1）准备——研究、组织、练习。2）了解听众——量身定制。3）管理焦虑——深呼吸、可视化、积极自我对话。4）肢体语言——眼神交流、自然手势。5）吸引听众——故事、例子、问题。感觉像对话的演讲最有效。

sentences.text: Five principles: 1) Preparation—research, organize, practice. 2) Know your audience—tailor your message. 3) Manage anxiety—deep breathing, visualization, positive self-talk. 4) Body language—eye contact, natural gestures, purposeful movement. 5) Engage your audience—stories, examples, questions. Speeches that feel like conversations are most effective.

questions.exp: 深呼吸、可视化和积极自我对话。
感觉像对话的演讲更有效。

title: Supply and Demand
transcript: Law of demand: price rises, quantity demanded falls. Law of supply: price rises, quantity supplied increases. Equilibrium: supply equals demand. Above equilibrium: surplus. Below equilibrium: shortage. Understanding these dynamics is crucial for market analysis.
transcript_cn: 需求法则：价格上涨，需求量下降。供给法则：价格上涨，供给量增加。均衡：供给等于需求。高于均衡：过剩。低于均衡：短缺。理解这些动态对市场分析至关重要。

sentences.text: Law of demand: price rises, quantity demanded falls. Law of supply: price rises, quantity supplied increases. Equilibrium: supply equals demand. Above equilibrium: surplus. Below equilibrium: shortage. Understanding these dynamics is crucial for market analysis.

title: Effective Learning Strategies
transcript: Research shows several strategies improve learning. Spaced repetition—reviewing material at increasing intervals—outperforms cramming. Active recall—testing yourself—strengthens memory more than re-reading. The Pomodoro Technique—25 minutes focused work plus 5-minute breaks—maintains concentration. Teaching others (the Feynman Technique) deepens understanding. Sleep consolidation means studying before sleep improves retention.
transcript_cn: 研究表明几种策略改善学习。间隔重复优于突击复习。主动回忆比重读更能增强记忆。番茄工作法保持专注。教别人（费曼技巧）加深理解。睡眠巩固意味着睡前学习改善记忆保持。

sentences.text: Research shows several strategies improve learning. Spaced repetition—reviewing material at increasing intervals—outperforms cramming. Active recall—testing yourself—strengthens memory more than re-reading. The Pomodoro Technique—25 minutes focused work plus 5-minute breaks—maintains concentration. Teaching others (the Feynman Technique) deepens understanding. Sleep consolidation means studying before sleep improves retention.

questions.exp: 间隔重复优于突击复习。

title: Cross-Cultural Communication
transcript: Effective cross-cultural communication requires awareness of cultural differences in communication styles. High-context cultures (Japan, China) rely on implicit messages and context. Low-context cultures (US, Germany) prefer direct, explicit communication. Nonverbal cues—gestures, eye contact, personal space—vary significantly across cultures. Active listening, avoiding assumptions, and asking clarifying questions are essential skills for cross-cultural interactions.
transcript_cn: 有效的跨文化沟通需要意识到文化差异。高语境文化（日本、中国）依赖隐含信息和语境。低语境文化（美国、德国）偏好直接明确的沟通。非语言信号——手势、眼神、个人空间——在不同文化中差异很大。积极倾听、避免假设和询问澄清问题是跨文化交流的必备技能。

Active listening, avoiding assumptions, and asking clarifying questions are essential skills for cross-cultural interactions.

transcript:
A: How to get to the engineering hall?

A: Near parking?
B: Yes, next to east lot. Has a distinctive dome on top.
A: I have class there at 2. Walk or shuttle?
B: 6<8?'Walking is fine.':'Take the shuttle from that stop.'}
A: Thanks!

A: 谢谢！
